phases.phase_goal: Frame the business question, decision boundary, governance limits, and public operational identity of the project.
phases.portfolio_meaning: This phase answers why the project exists, what responsible retention screening means, and why the repo is positioned as a decision-support app rather than an automated HR engine.
key_takeaways: The public repo identity is app-layer and deployment-friendly.
The project question is preserved as early attrition exposure review, not automated employment action.
Public operational truth must remain distinct from local rerun evidence.
phases.future_navigator_use: Support top-level navigator orientation, repo identity explanation, and governance-safe entry routing.

phases.phase_goal: Explain workforce patterns, EDA structure, and row-level or department-level exploratory signals that motivate intervention review.
phases.portfolio_meaning: This phase captures the evidence and pattern-reading layer behind retention screening, including workload, tenure, department, and salary context.
key_takeaways: The app can analyze either artifact-backed row truth or fallback screening heuristics.
EDA visuals are stable references, not recomputed runtime truth.
Department exposure can come from a generated summary artifact or filtered row aggregation depending on page state.
phases.future_navigator_use: Support source-aware drilldowns that explain which analytical layer a user is viewing and whether it is artifact-backed or fallback-derived.

phases.phase_goal: Represent model building, artifact generation, threshold selection logic, and explainability generation as governed build outputs.
phases.portfolio_meaning: This phase captures the modeling and artifact-construction spine behind the public story, while acknowledging that current repo behavior mixes declared provenance with local builder implementation.
key_takeaways: The repo’s builder is offline and artifact-generating, not a runtime training system.
Public reference selection is preserved even when local rerun comparison differs.
Explainability is present as generated SHAP outputs plus static presentation fallbacks.
phases.future_navigator_use: Support future source-of-truth resolution, artifact lineage display, and build-governance explanation before any agentic automation is layered on.

phases.phase_goal: Present the operational decision-support workflow that managers or reviewers would use to inspect exposure, threshold tradeoffs, and explainability outputs.
phases.portfolio_meaning: This phase is the current app’s strongest realized layer: it turns cleaned data and generated artifacts into a deployable review interface while preserving responsible-use messaging.
key_takeaways: Current runtime is designed to stay lightweight and artifact-backed.
Fallback mode exists for resilience but must remain clearly labeled.
The public app is a review and communication surface, not a training or decision automation surface.
phases.future_navigator_use: Support page-to-source explanation, source-aware user guidance, and future routing between review tasks, governance tasks, and offline build tasks.
